sections.citation: 8c63f059-cd39-4bdb-a74c-0a2fce79476d, Page Location Text in previous version  Text in amended version, p. 1
sections.text: 3 Section 6.2 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information required in Section 6.1 of this Standard and Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor and/or importer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor and/or importer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor and/or importer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

page_spans.text: 3 Section 6.2 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information required in Section 6.1 of this Standard and Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor and/or importer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer, packer, distributor and/or importer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to Cocoa (Cacao) Mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor, and Cocoa Cake, as defined, for the use in the manufacture of cocoa and chocolate products. These products may also be sold directly to the consumer.
sections.retrieval_text:

level=1

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CXG 21-1997).
sections.retrieval_text:
HYGIENE
number=5
level=1
It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CXG 21-1997).

page_spans.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Cocoa (Cacao) Mass (Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor)
sections.text: Cocoa (Cacao) Mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor is the product obtained from cocoa nib, which is obtained from cocoa beans of merchantable quality which have been cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as is technically possible with/without roasting, and with/without removal or addition of any of its constituents.

page_spans.text: Cocoa (Cacao) Mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor is the product obtained from cocoa nib, which is obtained from cocoa beans of merchantable quality which have been cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as is technically possible with/without roasting, and with/without removal or addition of any of its constituents.

sections.title: Cocoa cake
sections.text: Cocoa Cake is the product obtained by partial or complete removal of fat from cocoa nib or cocoa mass.

sections.title: Acidity regulators and emulsifiers
sections.text: Acidity regulators and emulsifiers used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 05.1.1 (Cocoa mixes (powders) and cocoa mass/cake) and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.  Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.

Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.

Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.

sections.title: Flavourings
sections.text: The flavourings used in products covered by this standard should comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008). Only those flavourings that do not imitate chocolate or milk flavours are permitted at GMP.

sections.title: Name of the product
sections.text: The name used to describe the product defined under Section 2.1 and complying with provisions of Section 3.1 of the Standard include “Cocoa (Cacao) Mass”, “Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor”, “Cocoa Paste”, “Unsweetened Chocolate” and “Bitter Chocolate”. In the case of products traded internationally, the name of the product shall be acceptable to the importing authorities. The name of the product described under Section 2.2 and complying with provisions of Section 3.2 of the Standard shall be: “Cocoa Cake”.

In the case of products traded internationally, the name of the product shall be acceptable to the importing authorities. The name of the product described under Section 2.2 and complying with provisions of Section 3.2 of the Standard shall be: “Cocoa Cake”.

The name of the product described under Section 2.2 and complying with provisions of Section 3.2 of the Standard shall be: “Cocoa Cake”.

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: Cocoa (Cacao) Mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor is the product obtained from cocoa nib, which is obtained from cocoa beans of merchantable quality which have been cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as is technically possible with/without roasting, and with/without removal or addition of any of its constituents.

prefilter.reason: Descriptive definition related to product composition; relevant for regulations.

classification.sentence: Cocoa (Cacao) Mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor is the product obtained from cocoa nib, which is obtained from cocoa beans of merchantable quality which have been cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as is technically possible with/without roasting, and with/without removal or addition of any of its constituents.
classification.function: Describes product definition with conditions on processing
classification.pattern: Definition statement with embedded conditional qualifications

components.processing_conditions: cleaned and freed from shells as thoroughly as is technically possible with/without roasting, and with/without removal or addition of any of its constituents

classification.justification: Product definition expressed with multiple conditional processing qualifiers not matching normative obligation or permission templates.

sentences.original: Cocoa Shell and Germ - not more than 5% m/m calculated on the fat-free dry matter  or  - not more than 1.75% calculated on an alkali free basis  (for Cocoa Shell only)  Cocoa Butter - 47- 60% m/m

classification.sentence: Cocoa Shell and Germ - not more than 5% m/m calculated on the fat-free dry matter  or  - not more than 1.75% calculated on an alkali free basis  (for Cocoa Shell only)  Cocoa Butter - 47- 60% m/m

classification.justification: Sets compositional quantitative limits with multiple criteria, no exact template match for this complex listing.

classification.pattern: Declarative obligation specifying naming
classification.justification: Strong normative obligation with clear structure prescribing product name, matching typical naming obligation templates.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR COCOA (CACAO) MASS (COCOA/CHOCOLATE LIQUOR) AND COCOA CAKE
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento de norma com título principal em caixa alta. Dois títulos principais foram identificados: '2022 Amendment' e um texto extenso que contém 'Page Location Text in previous version Text in amended version'. Ambos contêm texto explicativo associado, sem continuidade entre páginas.
A página contém apenas um cabeçalho repetitivo sem títulos ou texto adicional a ser estruturado para RAG.

A página apresenta uma estrutura clara de seções numeradas de nível 1 (1. SCOPE, 2. DESCRIPTIONS, 3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS, 4. FOOD ADDITIVES, 5. HYGIENE), com subseções de nível 2 numeradas em 2.x, 3.x e 4.x, associando textos explicativos diretamente aos seus respectivos títulos. Nenhuma seção continua de página anterior ou para próxima. Página contém cabeçalho repetitivo "CXS 141-1983  3" excluído do texto estrutural.

Página com duas seções principais numeradas de nível 1 (6. LABELLING e 7. METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING). A seção 6 contém subseções 6.1 e 6.2 com textos explicativos. A seção 7 contém subseções 7.1, 7.2 e 7.3, todas com textos muito curtos. A seção 6 aparenta continuar na próxima página (continues_on_next_page=true). Cabeçalho repetitivo detectado e excluído do conteúdo.
